service.education.title: Full system training across all modules
service.education.lead: We deliver practical training for your whole team based on real workflows: dashboard, calendar, documents, tasks, finance planning, business processes, production, warehouse and employee cards.
service.education.p1: Role-based onboarding: what managers, operators, executives and admins do in the system every day.
service.education.p2: Hands-on sessions with your own scenarios and data, not abstract examples.
service.education.p3: Learning pack: session recordings, checklists, step-by-step instructions and internal FAQ templates.
service.education.p4: Final validation and follow-up support: we verify that the team can independently complete key operations.
service.education.modulesLead: Each module includes navigation, key actions, typical mistakes and control points for team leads.
service.education.m1Body: KPIs, revenue blocks, plan/fact, quick navigation from summary widgets to operational sections.
service.education.m2Body: Weekly/monthly planning, event scheduling, linked tasks and workload balancing by day.
service.education.m3Body: Folders, categories, document sources, naming standards, quick retrieval and handover rules.
service.education.m4Body: Table/Kanban/Gantt workflow, statuses, assignees, priorities, deadlines and module linkage.
service.education.m5Body: Budget periods, request-based planning, statuses, reconciliation and discipline of data updates.
service.education.m6Body: Templates, in-progress and completed flows, step control and process ownership responsibilities.
service.education.m7Body: Production monitor, queues, work-in-progress, pause/complete states, output and defect tracking.
service.education.m8Body: Stock balances, nomenclature, journals and revisions, and daily warehouse control routines.
service.education.m9Body: Employee cards, org structure, roles and contacts, accountability zones and communication hygiene.
service.education.resultsTitle: What your team gets after training
service.education.o1: Unified operating standard for all departments and roles.
service.education.o2: Faster onboarding of new employees through ready checklists and recordings.
service.education.o3: Fewer process errors and less manual chaos in tasks, production and warehouse operations.
service.education.o4: Transparent responsibility and measurable discipline in daily system usage.
service.education.outro: We adapt depth, pace and examples to your company size, role matrix and current implementation stage.
service.investors.title: Invest in the operating system for business processes
service.investors.lead: Taska is building a modular business management platform for Uzbekistan and the region: CRM + operations + finance + production + warehouse — in one controllable environment.
service.investors.thesisLead: We combine fast custom implementation with a product platform approach: deliver value early and scale with reusable modules.
service.investors.th1Title: Modular platform, not a one-off project
service.investors.th1Body: CRM/ERP contours, tasks, finance planning, production and warehouse are assembled as modules; each implementation increases reusable product coverage.
service.investors.th2Body: We embed processes, roles and accountability. Adoption is a product feature: training, checklists, and measurable daily usage.
service.investors.th3Body: SMB and mid-market in the region often need fast customization, bilingual UI and pragmatic integrations instead of heavyweight “boxes”.
service.investors.whyNowLead: The market is ready for process-first automation and better execution control — without overpaying for unused enterprise suites.
service.investors.wn1: Growing complexity of operations: sales, projects, finance and production must be connected end-to-end.
service.investors.wn2: Companies demand transparency: plan/fact, deadlines, ownership and KPI dashboards.
service.investors.wn3: SMB adoption accelerates when systems are deployed in weeks and adapted to existing workflows.
service.investors.metricsBody: We share pipeline, active implementations, retention/usage signals, unit economics and case studies under NDA. This public page intentionally avoids disclosing sensitive commercial details.
service.investors.modelLead: Revenue combines implementation with recurring value: growth comes from module expansion and standardized rollout.
service.investors.bm1Body: Paid discovery, configuration, migration and team onboarding with a clear scope and acceptance criteria.
service.investors.bm2Body: Support, SLA, and iterative improvements; predictable recurring revenue and expansion via new modules.
service.investors.bm3Body: Process templates and module packs reduce delivery cost and time-to-value, improving margins over time.
service.investors.mo1: Unified data model and navigation: one workspace for departments instead of siloed tools.
service.investors.mo2: Process-centric UI: tasks, calendar, documents, finance and operations are linked, not scattered.
service.investors.mo3: Implementation playbook: role matrix, training program and adoption controls reduce failure risk.
service.investors.mo4: Modular roadmap: we can start small and expand, keeping ROI visible at every step.
service.investors.rd1Body: Strengthen core modules, standardize templates, and improve analytics dashboards for leadership.
service.investors.rd2Body: Expand production/warehouse routines, add reporting packs, and improve integration connectors.
service.investors.rd3Body: Scale delivery capacity, refine module packaging, and launch partner-led implementations.
service.investors.askTitle: Investment ask and use of funds
service.investors.askLead: We are open to strategic and financial investors. Under NDA we provide a detailed plan, budget and milestones for the round.
service.investors.ask1Body: Speed up module roadmap, quality, security and integrations while keeping platform coherence.
service.investors.ask2Body: Build repeatable acquisition: content, partners, sales playbooks and reference cases.
service.investors.ask3Body: Grow implementation capacity, training, and customer success to protect retention and expansion.
service.investors.outro: If you represent a fund or a strategic investor, send a request with “investment”. We will share a deck and arrange a call. NDA is available.
service.partners.title: Partner program for integrators and studios
service.partners.lead: If you implement business systems for clients, Taska gives you a modular platform, delivery playbooks, training and engineering support — so you can ship faster and earn on repeatable rollouts.
service.partners.whoLead: We work with teams who take responsibility for the client outcome and can run implementation end-to-end.
service.partners.w1Body: Process mapping, change management, KPI discipline, and controlled rollout.
service.partners.w2Body: System delivery for SMB/mid-market: CRM + operations + finance + production/warehouse.
service.partners.w3Body: Strong domain knowledge and hands-on client work; ready to follow a delivery standard.
service.partners.getLead: We provide the product platform and help you deliver consistently.
service.partners.g1Body: CRM + tasks + calendar + documents + finance planning + production + warehouse — assembled per client.
service.partners.g2Body: Integration guidance and engineering support: accounting, telephony, banks, marketplaces — by spec.
service.partners.g3Body: Solution consulting, escalation channel, and help with complex process cases.
service.partners.g4Body: Onboarding for your key specialists: product, roles, deployment routines and best practices.
service.partners.modelsLead: Choose a model that fits your sales and delivery capabilities.
service.partners.pm1Body: You bring the lead, we handle delivery. Revenue share is agreed per deal.
service.partners.pm2Body: You deliver implementation using our platform, we provide product support and roadmap alignment.
service.partners.pm3Body: For mature teams: co-branded or white-label delivery with defined responsibilities and SLA.
service.partners.on1Body: We align on your client profile, industries, delivery approach and target modules.
service.partners.on2Body: We train 1–3 key specialists and provide standard checklists and implementation routines.
service.partners.on3Body: We run the first rollout with shared control points: scope, acceptance and adoption metrics.
service.partners.r1: Ability to map processes and translate them into system configuration.
service.partners.r2: Readiness to run structured delivery: scope, milestones, acceptance criteria and training.
service.partners.r3: Client communication discipline and transparent reporting.
service.partners.r4: Respect for data security, access control and confidentiality.
service.partners.f1A: In some cases we can share inbound leads, but the core model is joint go-to-market and mutual referrals.
service.partners.f2Q: What industries do you focus on?
service.partners.f2A: We prioritize operationally complex SMB/mid-market: services, sales teams, production, logistics and multi-department operations.
service.partners.f3Q: How fast can we launch a client?
service.partners.f3A: It depends on scope, but our goal is week-based rollout with visible ROI and expansion via modules.
service.partners.f4Q: Can we customize the system deeply?
service.partners.f4A: Yes. We combine configuration, templates and targeted engineering changes — with change control and maintainability.
service.partners.outro: Write about your company and typical clients — we’ll propose the best partnership model and next steps.
